string.text:
The Pistol Mitraliera model 1963 (PM md. 63) is a Romanian assault rifle adopted by the Romanian military in the 1960s. It is a domestic variant of the AK-47 rifle, incorporating design elements from its Soviet counterpart. The PM md. 63 is known for its reliability and durability, functioning well even in adverse conditions. It features selective fire, chambered for the 7.62x39mm cartridge, and has a wooden stock, pistol grip, and handguard with a stamped steel receiver. With its standard 30-round detachable box magazine, the PM md. 63 has an effective range of several hundred meters, making it suitable for various combat scenarios. Over the years, the PM md. 63 has undergone modifications resulting in different sub-variants with distinct features, such as different stocks and sights. It has served as a reliable firearm for the Romanian armed forces and has seen deployment in various conflicts and operations.
 The PM md. 63's reputation for robustness and its close ties to the AK-47 platform have made it a widely recognized and utilized rifle both within Romania and beyond.

 %c[ui_gray_2]ATTACHMENTS:
 %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] Scopes: Most scopes for dovetail mounts except for the PSO series with eye-cups
 %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] Suppressor: PBS-1 Suppressor

The Pistol Mitraliera model 1963 (PM md. 63) is a Romanian assault rifle adopted by the Romanian military in the 1960s. It is a domestic variant of the AK-47 rifle, incorporating design elements from its Soviet counterpart. The PM md. 63 is known for its reliability and durability, functioning well even in adverse conditions. It features selective fire, chambered for the 7.62x39mm cartridge, and has a wooden stock, pistol grip, and handguard with a stamped steel receiver. With its standard 30-round detachable box magazine, the PM md. 63 has an effective range of several hundred meters, making it suitable for various combat scenarios. Over the years, the PM md. 63 has undergone modifications resulting in different sub-variants with distinct features, such as different stocks and sights. It has served as a reliable firearm for the Romanian armed forces and has seen deployment in various conflicts and operations.
 The MD-95 designation has an under-foldable stock compared to a rigid wooden one.

 %c[ui_gray_2]ATTACHMENTS:
 %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] Scopes: Most scopes for dovetail mounts except for the PSO series with eye-cups
 %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] Suppressor: PBS-1 Suppressor

The Pistol Mitraliera model 1990 (PM md. 90) is a Romanian assault rifle that was developed as a variant of the AKM and adopted by the Romanian military in 1990. It is commonly referred to as the Romanian AK-47 or AKM. The PM md. 90 retains the reliable and durable design of the AKM, chambered for the 7.62x39mm cartridge. It features selective fire capabilities, allowing for both semi-automatic and fully automatic modes of fire. The rifle is equipped with a wooden stock, pistol grip, and handguard, complementing its stamped steel receiver. With a standard detachable box magazine that holds 30 rounds, the PM md. 90 is effective at engaging targets at several hundred meters and is well-suited for various combat situations. The PM md. 90 has become a widely used firearm within the Romanian military and has seen active deployment in different conflicts and operations. Its reputation for reliability and durability, inherited from the AKM, has made it a preferred choice among armed forces. The Romanian variant maintains the iconic features of the AK platform while being customized to suit the requirements of the Romanian military, ensuring its effectiveness in the field.

 %c[ui_gray_2]ATTACHMENTS:
 %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] Scopes: Most scopes for dovetail mounts except for the PSO series with eye-cups
 %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] Suppressor: PBS-1 Suppressor
